# WattSec+ — Full LLM Reference

Consolidated architecture, code schema, and conventions. For quick navigation see llms.txt.

---

## Project overview

WattSec+ is a macOS menu bar application that displays real-time system power draw (watts), battery percentage with charge indicator, and system uptime. It is a fork of beutton/wattsec with an expanded feature set.

Key properties:
- Pure AppKit (no SwiftUI, no Xcode project)
- Builds with `swiftc` directly via `build.sh`
- Minimum deployment target: macOS 13.0
- App Sandbox disabled (required for IOKit SMC access — not App Store distributable)
- LSUIElement: true (no Dock icon, no app switcher entry)

---

## File map

```
wattsecplus/
├── WattSec/
│   ├── main.swift             # AppKit bootstrap — NSApplication + AppDelegate
│   ├── WattSecApp.swift       # All app logic: AppDelegate, PowerMonitor
│   ├── SettingsWindow.swift   # NSWindowController settings panel
│   ├── SMC.swift              # IOKit SMC interface
│   ├── Info.plist             # __VERSION__ substituted by build.sh via sed
│   ├── WattSec.entitlements   # Sandbox off; login items + automation on
│   └── Assets.xcassets/       # AppIcon (iconutil → AppIcon.icns in build)
├── build.sh                   # swiftc build + .app assembly, optional DMG/signing
├── VERSION                    # Single-line version string e.g. "2025.03.05"
├── README.md                  # User-facing documentation
├── CLAUDE.md                  # Architecture notes for AI assistants
├── llms.txt                   # LLM navigation index (this file's sibling)
├── llms-full.txt              # This file
└── docs/
    ├── SETUP.md               # Build prerequisites and commands
    ├── USAGE.md               # Settings and menu bar interactions
    └── CONTRIBUTING.md        # How to add features and modify the codebase
```

---

## UserDefaults schema

All keys are read/written via `UserDefaults.standard`. Settings version key: `settingsVersion` (currently `1`). Bump in `loadUserPreferences()` to force a reset to defaults on next launch.

| Key | Type | Default | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `detailLevel` | Int | 1 | 0 = low (0dp), 1 = medium (1dp), 2 = high (2dp) |
| `paceLevel` | Int | 1 | 1 = 1s, 3 = 3s, 5 = 5s |
| `widthMode` | String | "dynamic" | "dynamic", "fixed" |
| `fontSize` | Int | 2 | 0 = 10pt, 1 = 12pt, 2 = 12.5pt, 3 = 13pt, 4 = 15pt |
| `labelCase` | String | "lowercase" | "uppercase", "lowercase" |
| `showUptime` | Bool | true | — |
| `uptimeCompact` | Bool | false | removes spaces between uptime components |
| `uptimeShowMinutes` | Bool | false | show minutes even when showing days |
| `showBattery` | Bool | false | — |
| `showDash` | Bool | false | uses " - " separator in grouped mode |
| `groupStatusItems` | Bool | true | grouped vs. separate NSStatusItems |

Launch at Login is managed via `SMAppService.mainApp` — not stored in UserDefaults.

---

## Enums (WattSecApp.swift)

```swift
enum DetailLevel: Int  { case low = 0, medium = 1, high = 2 }
enum PaceLevel: Int    { case fast = 1, medium = 3, slow = 5 }
enum WidthMode: String { case dynamic, fixed }
enum LabelCase: String { case uppercase, lowercase }
enum FontSize: Int     { case extraSmall=0, small=1, medium=2, large=3, extraLarge=4 }
// FontSize.pointSize: 10, 12, 12.5, 13, 15
```

---

## AppDelegate — key properties (WattSecApp.swift)

```swift
class AppDelegate: NSObject, NSApplicationDelegate {
    // Status items — up to three, independent for Command-drag reordering
    private var statusItemWattage: NSStatusItem?
    private var statusItemBattery: NSStatusItem?
    private var statusItemUptime:  NSStatusItem?

    // In-memory settings (mirrors UserDefaults)
    private var detailLevel: DetailLevel
    private var paceLevel: PaceLevel
    private var widthMode: WidthMode
    private var fontSize: FontSize
    private var labelCase: LabelCase
    private var showUptime: Bool
    private var uptimeCompact: Bool
    private var uptimeShowMinutes: Bool
    private var showBattery: Bool
    private var showDash: Bool
    private var groupStatusItems: Bool

    // Battery cache — IOKit read once at launch and on plug/unplug events
    private var cachedBattery: BatteryState?

    // Font/dot image cache — invalidated on fontSize change
    private var _cachedFont: NSFont?
    private var _cachedDotImages: [NSColor: NSImage]

    // Settings window
    private var settingsWindowController: SettingsWindowController?
}
```

---

## PowerMonitor (WattSecApp.swift)

```swift
class PowerMonitor: NSObject, ObservableObject {
    static let shared = PowerMonitor()
    @Published private(set) var wattage: Double = 0.0
    @Published private(set) var lastReadFailed: Bool = false
    // Polling via DispatchSourceTimer on background queue "com.oliverbagley.WattSecPlus.smc"
    // Always dispatches @Published updates back to DispatchQueue.main
    func updatePace(_ pace: PaceLevel)  // rebuilds timer; no-op if pace unchanged
    func fetchWattage()                 // immediate one-shot read (called at launch)
}
```

---

## Display pipeline

1. `PowerMonitor.$wattage` → debounced (0.1s) Combine sink in AppDelegate
2. Calls `updateWattageDisplay()` on main thread
3. In **grouped** mode: calls `buildMenuBarAttributedString()` → sets on `statusItemWattage.button.attributedTitle`; removes battery + uptime status items entirely
4. In **separated** mode: updates each status item independently; battery/uptime items created on demand

### buildMenuBarAttributedString()

Public method on AppDelegate. Returns the exact attributed string shown in the menu bar (Battery · Wattage · Uptime order). Also called by SettingsWindow for the live preview.

```swift
func buildMenuBarAttributedString() -> NSAttributedString {
    // separator: "" if uptimeCompact, " - " if showDash, else " "
    // components: [battery?] [sep] [wattage] [sep] [uptime?]
    // each component rendered with its own sub-fonts (unit chars at 70–75% size)
}
```

### Attributed string helpers

- `wattageAttributedString(from:)` — number at full size, w/W at 70%
- `batteryAttributedString()` — optional dot (orange=charging, white=plugged) + number + % at 70% in lowercase mode
- `uptimeAttributedString()` — d/h/m labels at 75%, respects compact and showMinutes

---

## SettingsWindow ↔ AppDelegate communication

SettingsWindowController holds a `weak var appDelegate: AppDelegate?`.

When a control changes:
1. SettingsWindow writes the new value to UserDefaults
2. Calls `appDelegate?.applyChange(forKey:)` with the relevant key
3. AppDelegate reads the new value from UserDefaults, updates its in-memory property, invalidates caches if needed (font/dot images on `fontSize`; fixed width recalc on `fontSize` or `widthMode`), and calls `updateWattageDisplay()`

Launch at Login changes go via `appDelegate?.applyLaunchAtLoginChange()` which delegates to the existing `toggleLaunchAtLogin()` method (SMAppService error handling + menu sync).

---

## Fixed-width mode

`widestWidths: [DetailLevel: [Int: CGFloat]]` — precomputed pixel widths keyed by detail level → character count. Recalculated via `calculateWidestWidths()` on launch and on font/widthMode change.

When wattage crosses 100W (`Constants.highWattageThreshold`), the status item snaps to the wider slot. After dropping back below, it holds for 60 seconds (`Constants.highWattageTimeoutSeconds`) before returning to narrow — avoids rapid jitter.

---

## SettingsWindowController (SettingsWindow.swift)

```swift
final class SettingsWindowController: NSWindowController {
    weak var appDelegate: AppDelegate?

    // Controls
    var detailControl, paceControl, widthControl: NSSegmentedControl
    var fontSizeControl, labelCaseControl: NSSegmentedControl
    var showBatteryCheck, showUptimeCheck: NSButton
    var uptimeCompactCheck, uptimeMinutesCheck: NSButton
    var groupItemsCheck, showDashCheck, launchAtLoginCheck: NSButton
    var previewLabel: NSTextField   // shows buildMenuBarAttributedString() live

    // Live preview subscription
    var wattageSubscription: AnyCancellable?  // PowerMonitor.$wattage → refreshPreview()

    // Escape key handler
    var escMonitor: Any?  // local NSEvent monitor, removed in deinit
}
```

Window: 360×730pt, `.hudWindow` NSVisualEffectView, `cornerRadius 12`, `titlebarAppearsTransparent`, `fullSizeContentView`. Fade-in on `showWindow(_:)` via NSAnimationContext (0.18s easeOut).

`syncControls()` — reads all UserDefaults + live SMAppService state, called at init and each time the window is shown.

`refreshPreview()` — calls `appDelegate?.buildMenuBarAttributedString()`, adds `.center` paragraph style, sets `previewLabel.attributedStringValue`.

`updateUptimeSubOptions()` — disables/fades compact+minutes rows when Show Uptime is off.

---

## SMC.swift

Thin IOKit wrapper. Private singleton init (`SMC.shared`) — never create a second instance. `getValue(_ key: String) -> Double?` reads the `flt ` (4-byte IEEE 754) data type. Key used: `"PSTR"` (system power draw in watts). Negative values from charge handoffs are clamped to 0 in PowerMonitor.

---

## Build system

`build.sh` — pure bash, no Xcode project.

Key variables:
- `src_files` array — all `.swift` source files passed to `swiftc`
- `sdk_path` — resolved via `xcrun --sdk macosx --show-sdk-path`
- Frameworks: `Cocoa`, `IOKit`, `ServiceManagement`
- `-target arm64-apple-macos13.0` (or x86_64, or both via lipo for universal)
- Version: read from `VERSION` file; substituted into `Info.plist` via `sed s/__VERSION__/`
- Output: `dist/WattSecPlus.app` (dist/ is gitignored, fully regenerated each build)

To add a new source file: append it to the `src_files` array in `build.sh`.

---

## Key design constraints

- No SwiftUI — entry point is `main.swift` + `AppDelegate`. Never import SwiftUI.
- No Xcode project — do not add `.xcodeproj`. All compilation via `build.sh`.
- No second `SMC()` instance — `io_connect_t` is held persistently by `SMC.shared`.
- `updateMenuStates(_:selectedValue:)` uses typed `switch` for Int vs Int and String vs String comparisons — do not revert to `===` (breaks String-keyed checkmarks).
- `_cachedFont` and `_cachedDotImages` must be invalidated together in `changeFontSize(_:)`.
- `groupStatusItems` mode removes secondary NSStatusItems entirely (not just hides) — re-entering separated mode recreates them.
- `PowerMonitor.readAndPublish()` runs on `smcQueue`. Always dispatch back to main before touching `@Published` properties.
- Status item watcher timer fires on main run loop — do not add `DispatchQueue.main.async` inside its callback.
